figures.caption: Fig. 1. Deployment scenario of LEAN-3D. (a) Portable sensing devices acquire dense point clouds on a resource-constrained mobile platform. (b) Remote hosts for visualization or downstream processing.

Fig. 12. System-level streaming performance under posQ = 4 on KITTIDetection. (a) Mean end-to-end latency versus available bandwidth. (b) Output throughput versus available bandwidth. (c) Per-frame end-to-end latency at 8 MB/s, illustrating the difference in queue buildup over time.

Fig. 11. Qualitative reconstruction comparison under different quantization settings (posQ ∈ {4, 8, 16}) on KITTIDetection, where (a), (b), and (c) correspond to RENO, LEAN-3D, and ground truth, respectively. The reported PSNR is computed against the original raw point cloud. The identical PSNR values of RENO and LEAN-3D indicate that both codecs reconstruct the same quantized geometry, while the distortion is entirely introduced by the input quantization.

Fig. 10. Component-wise latency attribution of RENO and LEAN-3D on Jetson Orin Nano under different quantization settings (posQ ∈ {4, 8, 16}) on KITTIDetection. (a) Fast Occupancy Generator (FOG) vs. BPA latency. (b) Fast Coordinates Generator (FCG) vs. BCE latency. (c) Shallow level forward latency. (d) Shallow entropy encoding latency. (e) Deep level compression latency.
TABLE I REPRESENTATIVE LEARNED GEOMETRY CODECS, ENTROPY CODING, SETUP, AND RUNTIME. D: EFFECTIVE SPATIAL QUANTIZATION DEPTH (≡ OCTREE DEPTH / COORDINATE BIT DEPTH).
